aids.title: Four Logic Values
aids.content:
**0** = **O**ff (Ground)
**1** = **O**n (VCC)
**X** = e**X**tra confused (Unknown)
**Z** = **Z**ombie state (High-impedance, not driving)

Remember: 01XZ - 'Oh, One eXtra Zombie!'

aids.title: Wire vs Reg Rule
aids.content:
**W**ire = **W**ithout always blocks
**R**eg = **R**equired inside always blocks

If you're in an ALWAYS block, you ALWAYS need reg!
aids.forRemembering: When to use wire vs reg

aids.title: Number Format Trick
aids.content:
**Size'Base Value**

8'hFF = "8-bit hex FF"

Bases to remember:
- **b** = **b**inary (01)
- **o** = **o**ctal (0-7)
- **d** = **d**ecimal (0-9)
- **h** = **h**ex (0-F)

All bases are letters from their names!

aids.title: Vector Visualization
aids.content:
For wire [7:0] data:

```
Bit:  7  6  5  4  3  2  1  0
      |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
     MSB----------------->LSB
```

Think: **Left is HIGH**, like reading a number!
8'b10101010 = 170 (MSB on left)

aids.title: Memory Array Story
aids.content:
Think of `reg [7:0] mem [0:255]` as a **filing cabinet**:

- 256 drawers (addresses 0-255)
- Each drawer holds 8 bits
- `mem[5]` = open drawer 5
- `mem[5] = 8'hAB` = put AB in drawer 5

The first bracket is the drawer SIZE, second is NUMBER of drawers!

aids.title: Signed Data Rhyme
aids.content:
If negatives you need to see,
Add 'signed' explicitly!

`reg signed [7:0] temp;`
Now -128 to +127 you'll get!
